title: Mass spectrometry
summary: 9701 AS mass spectrometry — molecular ion peak, fragmentation patterns, and determining relative molecular mass.
learningObjectives: Describe ionisation, acceleration, deflection, and detection.
Identify the molecular ion peak [M]⁺ and its m/z value.
Predict common fragment ions for organic molecules.
Use isotope patterns (e.g. Cl, Br) to support assignment.

simpleExplanation.title: Mass of fragments
simpleExplanation.summary: Mass spectrometry measures m/z of ions. The highest significant peak often gives Mr; smaller peaks reveal fragmentation.
steps: Electron impact: M → M⁺ + e⁻ — molecular ion [M]⁺ at far right.
Fragmentation: loss of CH₃ (15), OH (17), CO (28) common.
Chlorine: M and M+2 peaks 3:1 ratio (³⁵Cl : ³⁷Cl).
Bromine: M and M+2 peaks ~1:1 (⁷⁹Br : ⁸¹Br).

sections.content: **Mass spectrometry** (22.2) complements **IR** (22.1). Practice reading **m/z** axes and calculating **Mr** from the molecular ion — then propose a structure consistent with both spectra.
The molecular ion is not always the **base peak** (tallest). Look for the **rightmost** significant peak and check **isotope clusters** before assigning a formula.
